name: The Arc of Illinois
description: State chapter of The Arc advocating for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities across Illinois.
address: 20 N. Wacker Dr., Suite 1540, Chicago, IL 60606

name: Illinois Early Intervention Program
description: Statewide early intervention services for infants and toddlers from birth to age 3 with developmental delays or disabilities.

name: Special Olympics Illinois
description: Year-round sports training and athletic competition for children and adults with intellectual disabilities in Illinois.

name: Lurie Children's Hospital - Developmental and Behavioral Pediatrics
description: Comprehensive developmental and behavioral pediatric evaluations and treatment at one of the top children's hospitals in the nation.

name: Equip for Equality
description: Illinois' federally designated protection and advocacy organization providing legal services and advocacy for people with disabilities.
address: 20 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 300, Chicago, IL 60602

name: Illinois Respite Coalition
description: Connects families of individuals with disabilities to respite care providers and resources across Illinois.

name: Illinois Assistive Technology Program (IATP)
description: Provides assistive technology device demonstrations, loans, and resources to individuals with disabilities throughout Illinois.
address: 1 W. Old State Capitol Plaza, Suite 100, Springfield, IL 62701

name: Illinois ABLE Account
description: Tax-advantaged savings accounts for eligible individuals with disabilities to save without affecting means-tested benefits.

name: Illinois Division of Developmental Disabilities
description: State agency providing services, support, and programs for individuals with developmental disabilities in Illinois.

name: Pace Paratransit Services
description: ADA paratransit services providing door-to-door transportation for individuals with disabilities in the Chicago suburban area.

name: Illinois Medicaid Home and Community-Based Waivers
description: Medicaid waiver programs providing home and community-based services for individuals with disabilities as an alternative to institutional care.

name: Camp Red Leaf
description: Residential summer camp for children and adults with developmental disabilities offering outdoor recreation and social activities.

name: Family Resource Center on Disabilities
description: Provides parent training, information, and support to help families navigate disability services and education systems.
address: 20 E. Jackson Blvd., Room 300, Chicago, IL 60604

name: Illinois Employment and Economic Opportunity for Persons with Disabilities Task Force
description: State programs and resources supporting competitive integrated employment for individuals with disabilities.
